body.title: Abort pe_chain at pe_read_ticket when ticket description is absent
body.result: Guard in pe_read_ticket aborts when description absent/title-only; 3 new tests
body.description:
pe_chain hallucinates HIGH-inertia file scope when a ticket has no description. SITUATE infers files from title semantics, OBSERVE reads them, HYPOTHESIZE edits them. The scope guard catches the symptom after 0 attempts but never stops the underlying cause.

Fix: abort at pe_read_ticket with basket[error] when description is absent or equals the title (title-only fallback). SITUATE never runs, hallucination never fires.

Guard condition: len(description) < 50 OR description.strip() == title.strip(). Error message: 'Ticket {id} has no description — add Affected files and scope before Igor can plan this.'

HIGH-INERTIA PRE-APPROVAL: pe_chain.py is HIGH-inertia. Change is a 4-line additive early-return guard in pe_read_ticket() only — no existing logic altered. Pre-approved by Akien: 'yes /fix it' (2026-05-10).

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain.py (pe_read_ticket, ~line 584)
**Design rules:** none apply beyond the HIGH-inertia pre-approval already stamped above
**Scope boundary:** In scope: one guard in pe_read_ticket(), one new test. Out of scope: changing SITUATE prompt, HYPOTHESIZE prompt, scope_guard logic, or any other pe_chain step.
**Test plan:** Add test: ticket with empty description → pe_read_ticket sets basket['error'] containing 'no description'; ticket with description == title → same; ticket with real description → proceeds normally. All existing pe_chain tests must pass.
